meaning: adj.谄媚的,讨人欢喜的,奉承的,(肖像等)比真容更美丽
examples: I knew she was just flattering me.
Most of his colleagues had positive, even flattering things to say.
It wasn't a very flattering photograph.
It was a "mediocre" performance and the 1-0 victory was "flattering" to the German side.
In the wake of Game 4, though, those comparisons are less flattering than they've ever been.
